Our experienced technicians safely eliminate hornet nests from residential and commercial properties throughout Piscataway, ensuring your peace of mind.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over a decade of experience to Piscataway residents, with licensed technicians who understand the unique pest challenges of Middlesex County. We offer same-day service, comprehensive nest removal, and preventative treatments to keep hornets from returning to your property.
localContext: From the historic neighborhoods near Rutgers University to the family homes along the Raritan River and commercial properties near the Piscataway Town Center, hornets can build nests in eaves, trees, and attics throughout our community. Piscataway's mix of wooded areas and suburban development creates ideal conditions for hornet activity, especially during spring and summer months.

faqs.question: How much does hornet removal cost in Piscataway, NJ?
faqs.answer: Hornet removal in Piscataway typically costs between $350 and $550, depending on nest location, size, and accessibility. We provide free inspections and upfront pricing before beginning any work, with no hidden fees.

faqs.question: How quickly can you remove a hornet nest in Piscataway?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and emergency hornet removal services throughout Piscataway. Most hornet nest removals are completed within 1-2 hours, and we'll ensure the area is completely safe before we leave your property.

faqs.question: Is hornet removal safe for my family and pets?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Our licensed technicians use EPA-approved products and proven safety protocols to eliminate hornets while protecting your family, pets, and property. We recommend keeping children and pets indoors during treatment and for 2-3 hours afterward.

faqs.question: What's the difference between hornets, wasps, and yellow jackets?
faqs.answer: Hornets are larger than wasps and yellow jackets, typically building large, enclosed paper nests in trees or under eaves. They're more aggressive when defending their nest. Our Piscataway technicians are trained to identify and safely remove all stinging insect species.
